DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFFS CHARLES HARB AND THE CHARLES HARB REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST'S MOTION COMPELLING THE CUSTODIAN/PMQ OF STATE FARM GENERAL INS. CO. TO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS AT DEPOSITION(S) - PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL INSURANCE POLICIES IS DENIED. THIS MATTER INVOLVES AN ALLEGATIONS OF NEGLIGENCE AGAINST AN INSURANCE AGENT RELATED TO COVERAGE OF DESTRUCTION OF A HOUSE, WHICH WAS INSURED THROUGH A HOMEOWNER'S POLICY. PLAINTIFF MOVES TO COMPEL OTHER INSURANCE POLICIES ISSUED BY STATE FARM TO PLAINTIFF UNRELATED TO THE PROPERTY AT ISSUE IN THE LITIGATION. PLAINTIFF ASSERTS THE OTHER POLICIES RELATE TO THE "RELATIONSHIP" BETWEEN THE ALLEGEDLY NEGLIGENT AGENT AND STATE FARM. HOWEVER, STATE FARM HAS CONCEDED THAT THE AGENT WAS ACTING AS STATE FARM'S AGENT AND THAT STATE FARM IS RESPONSIBLE FOR ANY VERDICT AGAINST THE AGENT. ACCORDINGLY, THE OTHER POLICIES ARE NOT RELEVANT AND NOT CALCULATED TO LEAD TO THE DISCOVERY OF ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER.) JUDGE PRO TEM: GEOFFREY HOWARD; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED. (302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS DEREK SENE & STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE TWO-YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS BARS PLAINTIFF'S ACTION. A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PROFESSIONAL NEGLIGENCE AGAINST AN INSURANCE AGENT DOES NOT ACCRUE UNTIL THE PLAINTIFF (1) SUSTAINS DAMAGE AND (2) DISCOVERS, OR SHOULD DISCOVER, THE NEGLIGENCE. SEE HYDRO-MILL CO. INC., V. HAYWARD, TILTON AND ROLAPP INSURANCE ASSOCIATES (2004) 115 CAL.APP.4TH 1145. IN THIS CASE, THE DAMAGE OCCURRED IN EARLY 2007 AND PLAINTIFF WAS AWARE OF DEFENDANTS' ALLEGED NEGLIGENCE AT AROUND THE SAME TIME WHEN HE WAS TOLD THAT HIS POLICY DID NOT COVER THE DEMOLITION DAMAGE. (PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSING SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED FACTS 4, 36, AND 37; PLAINTIFF'S (MR. HARB) DEC., PAR. 8; PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSING POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, 4:2-7). PLAINTIFF FILED THIS UNTIMELY ACTION ON SEPTEMBER 5, 2012. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185
MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-27-2014 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

Judge Pro Tem James Watson, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D302)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-14-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: HAROLD JAFFE, ESQ., 415.452.2610, FOR PLAINTIFFS.
STEPHEN ELLINGSON, ESQ., 650.637.9100, FOR DEFENDANTS.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS DEREK SENE & STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. The two-year statute of limitations bars plaintiff's action. A cause of action for professional negligence against an insurance agent does not accrue until the plaintiff (1) sustains damage and (2) discovers, or should discover, the negligence. See Hydro-Mill Co. Inc., v. Hayward, Tilton and Rolapp Insurance Associates (2004) 115 Cal.App.4th 1145. In this case, the damage occurred in early 2007 and Plaintiff was aware of defendants' alleged negligence at around the same time when he was told that his policy did not cover the demolition damage. (Plaintiff's Opposing Separate Statement of Undisputed Facts 4, 36, and 37; Plaintiff's (Mr. Harb) dec., par. 8; Plaintiff's Opposing Points and Authorities, 4:2-7). Plaintiff filed this untimely action on September 5, 2012. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Nov-20-2013 Continued To Jan-14-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn Compelling The Custodian/Pmq Of State Farm General Ins. Co. To Produce Documents At Deposition (S)
Off Calendar Dec-06-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its uncontested tentative ruling on PLAINTIFFs CHARLES HARB and THE CHARLES HARB REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST's Motion Compelling The Custodian/PMQ Of State Farm General Ins. Co. To Produce Documents At Deposition(s). Plaintiff's motion to compel insurance policies is denied. This matter involves an allegations of negligence against an insurance agent related to coverage of destruction of a house, which was insured through a homeowner's policy. Plaintiff moves to compel other insurance policies issued by State Farm to plaintiff unrelated to the property at issue in the litigation. Plaintiff asserts the other policies relate to the "relationship" between the allegedly negligent agent and State Farm. However, State Farm has conceded that the agent was acting as State Farm's agent and that State Farm is responsible for any verdict against the agent. Accordingly, the other policies are not relevant and not calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (Prevailing party to submit a proposed order.) Judge Pro Tem: Geoffrey Howard; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
